_note: SAMPLE DATA — for demonstration only. Replace with verified real news before public launch.

articles.title: Virginia Proposes New Zoning Rules for Data Centers in Northern Virginia
articles.summary: Loudoun County, already home to the world's largest concentration of data centers, is considering stricter setback and noise ordinances that would effectively pause new construction in some residential-adjacent zones.

articles.title: Senate Bill Would Require AI Impact Assessments Before Federal Contracts
articles.summary: A bipartisan group of senators introduced the AI Transparency in Procurement Act, requiring federal agencies to conduct algorithmic impact assessments before deploying AI systems on contracts exceeding $10 million.

articles.title: Texas Power Grid Authority Warns of Summer Strain from AI Demand Surge
articles.summary: ERCOT projects that AI and cryptocurrency data centers could add 40 GW of load by 2030, raising reliability concerns for the isolated Texas grid during extreme heat events.

articles.title: Microsoft Pauses Data Center Expansion in Oregon Amid Water Use Scrutiny
articles.summary: Microsoft has temporarily halted permitting for a new campus in Umatilla County after environmental groups filed a challenge citing the facility's projected 1.6 million gallon-per-day water draw from the Columbia River watershed.

articles.title: California AB 2013 Advances: Mandatory AI Training Data Disclosure
articles.summary: California's Assembly Bill 2013 passed committee with amendments, requiring AI developers deploying models in California to publish summaries of training datasets used in high-risk AI systems.

articles.title: Google Announces 20 GW Nuclear Power Deal to Fuel AI Data Centers
articles.summary: Alphabet's Google signed agreements with multiple nuclear energy providers to deliver carbon-free baseload power to its expanding network of AI inference and training facilities across the United States.

articles.title: NVIDIA Faces Export Scrutiny as New Chip Controls Take Effect
articles.summary: The Commerce Department's latest semiconductor export controls extend licensing requirements to additional H100-class GPU configurations, complicating NVIDIA's sales strategy in several markets while boosting domestic data center construction.

articles.title: Hood River County Reaffirms Data Center Ban After Public Hearing
articles.summary: Hood River County commissioners voted unanimously to maintain their 2012 ban on commercial data centers after a public hearing drew hundreds of residents opposed to loosening restrictions on agricultural and scenic land.

articles.title: AWS Announces $15B Investment in South Carolina Cloud Region
articles.summary: Amazon Web Services plans a new cloud availability zone anchored in Berkeley County, citing the state's favorable tax incentives, available land, and fiber corridor access along I-26.

articles.title: FTC Opens Investigation Into AI Model Training Data Practices
articles.summary: The Federal Trade Commission launched a formal inquiry into whether leading AI developers are obtaining training data through means that may constitute unfair or deceptive practices, targeting six companies for initial document requests.

articles.title: Iowa Expands Data Center Tax Exemption to Include AI Infrastructure
articles.summary: Iowa's legislature passed an expansion of its existing sales tax exemption for data center equipment to include dedicated AI inference hardware, positioning the state as a lower-cost alternative to coastal markets.

articles.title: Washington State PUD Lifts Moratorium on Chelan County Data Centers
articles.summary: Chelan County Public Utility District voted to end its two-year pause on new data center interconnection agreements after completing a grid upgrade study, though applicants will face a new tiered pricing structure tied to renewable capacity.
